purpose: Implement F5_ri_corrected friction closure: 1-parameter OLS Ri correction on F3_shah_apparent baseline; candidate screen only (R²<0 for all leg classes with 3-point data)
scope: Salt 2/3/4 Jin mainline operating points; non-upcomer spans; cfd-modeling-tools Fluid solver + test suite
key_finding: F5 ≡ F3_shah_apparent (c=0 for all non-upcomer leg classes; R²<0 for all with 3-point forced-intercept OLS). Ri correction does not improve on F3_shah_apparent with current Salt 2-4 data (narrow Ri range 0.38-1.38 per class).
open_questions_for_user: Is a 3-point forced-intercept OLS the right screen criterion, or should we use a 2-parameter fit (intercept free) or a physical form (e.g., Scheele-Hanratty correction)?
After AGENT-181 gate requalification, should F5 be re-fit automatically from expanded Ri range, or should the re-fit await a dedicated coordinator decision?

The phi_mean values (heater ≈ 1.81, cooler ≈ 1.54, downcomer ≈ 1.81) differ substantially across leg classes — does this suggest the dominant mechanism is flow-regime dependent rather than Ri-dependent?
